questions.questionText: An electric toaster has resistance of $$60 \Omega$$ at room temperature $$\left(27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$$. The toaster is connected to a $$220 \mathrm{~V}$$ supply. If the current flowing through it reaches $$2.75 \mathrm{~A}$$, the temperature attained by toaster is around : ( if $$\alpha=2 \times 10^{-4}$$/$$^\circ \mathrm{C}$$)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A potential divider circuit is shown in figure. The output voltage V$$_0$$ is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If n represents the actual number of deflections in a converted galvanometer of resistance G and shunt resistance S. Then the total current I when its figure of merit is K will be:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two cells of same emf but different internal resistances r1 and r2 are connected in series with a resistance R. The value of resistance R, for which the potential difference across second cell is zero, is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The length of a given cylindrical wire is increased to double of its original length. The percentage increase in the resistance of the wire will be ____________ %.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An electric power line having total resistance of $2 \Omega$, delivers 1 kW of power at 250 V . The percentage efficiency of transmission line is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A cylindrical conductor of length 2 m and area of cross-section $0.2 \mathrm{~mm}^2$ carries an electric current of 1.6 A when its ends are connected to a 2 V battery. Mobility of electrons in the conductor is $\alpha \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2 / \mathrm{V} . \mathrm{s}$. The value of $\alpha$ is :

(electron concentration $=5 \times 10^{28} / \mathrm{m}^3$ and electron charge $=1.6 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{C}$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The value of current i1 flowing from A to C in the circuit diagram is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Four resistances 40 $$\Omega $$, 60 $$\Omega $$, 90 $$\Omega $$ and 110 $$\Omega $$ make the arms of a quadrilateral ABCD. Across AC is a battery of emf 40 V and internal resistance negligible.The potential difference across BD in V is _______.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
With the help of potentiometer, we can determine the value of emf of a given cell. The sensitivity of the potentiometer is

(A) directly proportional to the length of the potentiometer wire

(B) directly proportional to the potential gradient of the wire

(C) inversely proportional to the potential gradient of the wire

(D) inversely proportional to the length of the potentiometer wire

Choose the correct option for the above statements :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: When two resistance $$\mathrm{R_1}$$ and $$\mathrm{R_2}$$ connected in series and introduced into the left gap of a meter bridge and a resistance of 10 $$\Omega$$ is introduced into the right gap, a null point is found at 60 cm from left side. When $$\mathrm{R_1}$$ and $$\mathrm{R_2}$$ are connected in parallel and introduced into the left gap, a resistance of 3 $$\Omega$$ is introduced into the right gap to get null point at 40 cm from left end. The product of $$\mathrm{R_1}$$ $$\mathrm{R_2}$$ is ____________$$\Omega^2$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A null point is found at 200 cm in potentiometer when cell in secondary circuit is shunted by 5$$\Omega$$. When a resistance of 15$$\Omega$$ is used for shunting, null point moves to 300 cm. The internal resistance of the cell is ___________$$\Omega$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Resistance of the wire is measured as 2 $$\Omega$$ and 3 $$\Omega$$ at 10$$^\circ$$C and 30$$^\circ$$C respectively. Temperature co-efficient of resistance of the material of the wire is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: All resistances in figure are 1 $$\Omega$$ each. The value of current 'I' is $${a \over 5}$$ A. The value of a is _________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An aluminium wire is stretched to make its length, 0.4% larger. The percentage change in resistance is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Due to cold weather a 1 m water pipe of cross-sectional area 1 cm<sup>2</sup> is filled with ice at $$-$$10$$^\circ$$C. Resistive heating is used to melt the ice. Current of 0.5A is passed through 4 k$$\Omega$$ resistance. Assuming that all the heat produced is used for melting, what is the minimum time required? (Given latent heat of fusion for water/ice = 3.33 $$\times$$ 10<sup>5</sup> J kg<sup>$$-$$1</sup>, specific heat of ice = 2 $$\times$$ 10<sup>3</sup> J kg<sup>$$-$$1</sup> and density of ice = 10<sup>3</sup> kg/m<sup>3</sup>
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two resistors R<sub>1</sub> = (4 $$\pm$$ 0.8) $$\Omega$$ and R<sub>2</sub> = (4 $$\pm$$ 0.4) $$\Omega$$ are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance of their parallel combination will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: What will be the most suitable combination of three resistors A = 2$$\Omega$$, B = 4$$\Omega$$, C = 6$$\Omega$$ so that $$\left( {{{22} \over 3}} \right)$$$$\Omega$$ is equivalent resistance of combination?
options: (A) Parallel combination of A and C connected in series with B.
(B) Parallel combination of A and B connected in series with C.
(C) Series combination of A and C connected in parallel with B.
(D) Series combination of B and C connected in parallel with A.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A potentiometer wire of length 10 m and resistance 20 $$\Omega$$ is connected in series with a 25 V battery and an external resistance 30 $$\Omega$$. A cell of emf E in secondary circuit is balanced by 250 cm long potentiometer wire. The value of E (in volt) is $${x \over {10}}$$. The value of x is __________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Ratio of thermal energy released in two resistors R and 3R connected in parallel in an electric circuit is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a metre bridge experiment the balance point is obtained if the gaps are closed by 2$$\Omega$$ and 3$$\Omega$$. A shunt of X $$\Omega$$ is added to 3$$\Omega$$ resistor to shift the balancing point by 22.5 cm. The value of X is ___________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the given figure, the emf of the cell is 2.2 V and if internal resistance is 0.6$$\Omega$$. Calculate the power dissipated in the whole circuit :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A wire of resistance R1 is drawn out so that its length is increased by twice of its original length. The ratio of new resistance to original resistance is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: As shown in the figure, a potentiometer wire of resistance $$20 \,\Omega$$ and length $$300 \mathrm{~cm}$$ is connected with resistance box (R.B.) and a standard cell of emf $$4 \mathrm{~V}$$. For a resistance '$$R$$' of resistance box introduced into the circuit, the null point for a cell of $$20 \,\mathrm{mV}$$ is found to be $$60 \mathrm{~cm}$$. The value of '$$R$$' is ___________ $$\Omega .$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Different combination of 3 resistors of equal resistance $$\mathrm{R}$$ are shown in the figures. The increasing order for power dissipation is:
questions.subject: Physics
